meaning: adj.荳榊粋騾ｻ霎醍噪,荳榊粋逅㋞噪
examples: There are some people who will use any kind of argument, no matter how illogical, so long as they can score off an opponent.
If I'm right, we should foster a creative spirit because a dose of illogical creativity will complement the rationality of the machine.
It was absurd and illogical to go out into such a storm.
Based on its own perceptions, an employee culture rarely does anything illogical or unpredictable.
Attitudes to children's research, even among regulatory bodies can be illogical and therefore confusing to parents.
